commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a 16 MiB bounded read inside OpenAI OAuth `postTokenForm`, keeps downstream bounded parsing, and adds oversized exchange/refresh regression tests.

PR surface: Source +23, Tests +59. Total +82 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main and `v2026.6.10` perform an unbounded first body read in `postTokenForm` before downstream parsing. I did not run a live token-endpoint repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is needed from this review; the remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of the auth-provider cap behavior.

target.title: fix(openai): bound OAuth token response read at first body read in postTokenForm to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The original fix (#97716) applied bounded response readers downstream in `exchangeAuthorizationCode` and `refreshAccessToken`, but those operate on a `Response` already re-wrapped after `postTokenForm` consumed the entire body with `await response.arrayBuffer()`. The actual OOM vector was the unbounded first read inside `postTokenForm`.

This PR moves the 16 MiB bounded read (`readResponseWithLimit`) into `postTokenForm` — before the body is buffered — so oversized OAuth token responses are cancelled mid-stream rather than fully buffered.

## Evidence

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw main @ 0ce10d7793

**Existing tests**: 10/10 pass (unchanged behavior preserved).

**New regression tests**: 2/2 oversized-response tests pass:
- `rejects oversized token exchange responses` — verifies exchange path catches overflow from postTokenForm
- `rejects oversized token refresh responses` — verifies refresh path catches overflow from postTokenForm

**Oversized test approach**: Creates an 18 MiB ReadableStream response (cap is 16 MiB), verifies the bounded reader cancels the stream and the error propagates as a formatted token failure.

## Risk

Low. The 16 MiB cap matches the codebase convention. The cap is applied at the first body read (inside `postTokenForm`), and downstream bounded readers provide defense-in-depth on the re-wrapped response.

Closes #97716

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
